visa_free.nationality: British nationals (Overseas), British overseas territories citizens, British overseas citizens, British protected persons, British subjects

eta.nationality: All Annex II visa-free nationalities (see visa_free list) - ETIAS pending
eta.notes: EES (Entry/Exit System) became operational progressively from 12 October 2025 and fully operational at all Schengen external borders on 10 April 2026 - replacing passport stamps with digital border records for non-EU nationals. EES does not require advance application; it is processed at the border. ETIAS (European Travel Information and Authorization System) is a separate advance travel authorization expected to launch in Q4 2026. Once ETIAS is active, nationals of Annex II visa-free countries will need to apply online and pay a small fee before entering the Schengen area. Mandatory enforcement not expected until at least April 2027 after a 6-month carrier/airline transition period. ETIAS not yet operational as of June 2026. Sources: home-affairs.ec.europa.eu (EES fully operational 10 April 2026); exteriores.gob.es Washington entry conditions page (ETIAS expected Q4 2026).

visa_policy.notes: Spain is a Schengen member state; visa policy is governed by EU Regulation 2018/1806 (consolidated version as of 2025-12-30). Annex II lists visa-free third countries (90 days/180-day period). EU/EEA/Swiss citizens have freedom of movement with no visa or stay limits. Nationals of countries in Annex I of the regulation require a Schengen visa. Notable recent changes: Vanuatu was removed from Annex II (effective 14 January 2025, Regulation EU 2025/11); Albania added in 2010 (biometric passports only); Kosovo added effective 1 January 2024 (biometric passports only, Regulation 2023/850). EES (Entry/Exit System) became progressively operational from 12 October 2025 and fully operational at all Schengen external borders on 10 April 2026. ETIAS electronic travel authorization soft launch expected Q4 2026; mandatory enforcement not before April 2027.

credential.subtype: any valid residence permit issued by a Schengen State

conditional_access.conditions: Holder of a valid residence permit issued by any Schengen State may move freely within other Schengen States (including Spain) for up to 90 days in any 180-day period without requiring a separate Schengen visa, provided they hold a valid travel document. Applies to nationals who would otherwise require a visa. Residence permit stay periods are not counted against the 90-day visa-free allowance. Authority: Article 21 of the Convention Implementing the Schengen Agreement; confirmed on Spain's official consular entry conditions page.

credential.subtype: valid long-stay visa (Type D national visa) issued by a Schengen State

conditional_access.conditions: Holder of a valid long-stay visa (Type D) issued by any Schengen State may move freely within other Schengen States (including Spain) for up to 90 days in any 180-day period without requiring a separate Schengen visa. Applies to nationals who would otherwise require a visa. Confirmed on Spain's official consular entry conditions page: 'This visa will not be necessary if the interested party has a valid residence permit or a valid long-term visa issued by a Schengen State.'

Holders of diplomatic and service/official passports issued by Algeria are exempt from Schengen visa requirements for Spain. Bilateral agreement recorded in EU OJ 2023/C 340/09 (27 September 2023) - Information on national derogations from the visa requirement under Regulation (EU) 2018/1806.

Holders of diplomatic and service/official passports issued by Armenia are exempt from Schengen visa requirements for Spain. Bilateral agreement recorded in EU OJ 2023/C 340/09 (27 September 2023).

Bilateral agreement recorded in EU OJ 2023/C 340/09 (27 September 2023). Spain's Washington Consulate cited Bolivia as an example of a diplomatic passport exemption.

Holders of diplomatic and service/official passports issued by Ecuador are exempt from Schengen visa requirements for Spain. Bilateral agreement signed in Quito on 20 November 2003 (BOE-A-2004-2217). Also recorded in EU OJ 2023/C 340/09.

Holders of Indian diplomatic passports do not need a visa for short-term stays in Spain (less than 90 days). Special passports also exempt per EU OJ 2023/C 340/09. Bilateral agreement recorded in EU OJ 2023/C 340/09 (27 September 2023). Note: ordinary Indian passport holders require a Schengen visa.

Holders of diplomatic, service and special passports issued by Oman are exempt from Schengen visa requirements for Spain. Bilateral agreement signed 5 November 2025, entered into force 7 January 2026 per Royal Decree 3/2026 (Oman Official Gazette). Confirmed by Spain's Ministry of Foreign Affairs on 5 January 2026. Also recorded in EU OJ 2023/C 340/09 (27 September 2023) for an earlier agreement covering diplomatic and service passports.
Holders of diplomatic and service/official passports issued by Pakistan are exempt from Schengen visa requirements for Spain. Bilateral agreement recorded in EU OJ 2023/C 340/09 (27 September 2023).

Holders of Qatari diplomatic and special passports are exempt from visa requirements for Spain for stays not exceeding 90 days within any 180-day period, provided no remunerated activities are undertaken. 'Acuerdo sobre supresión de los requisitos de visado para los titulares de pasaportes diplomáticos, especiales y de servicio entre el Reino de España y el Estado de Qatar, hecho en Madrid y Doha el 26 de octubre de 2020.' Entry into force: 12 November 2021 (BOE-A-2021-17459). Note: The agreement covers Spain's diplomatic and service passports and Qatar's diplomatic and special passports.

Holders of Saudi Arabian diplomatic, service and special passports are exempt from visa requirements for Spain. Bilateral 'Agreement on the Mutual Visa Exemption for Holders of Diplomatic, Special and Service Passports' signed in Madrid on 15 May 2026 during the visit of Saudi Foreign Minister Prince Faisal bin Farhan bin Abdullah. Confirmed by multiple official-adjacent reports (Saudi Gazette, Zawya, Spain-Saudi bilateral communiqués). Not yet recorded in an EU OJ update or BOE as of June 2026. BOE publication pending ratification. Retain with caution pending BOE.

Holders of diplomatic passports issued by Togo are exempt from Schengen visa requirements for Spain. Bilateral agreement recorded in EU OJ 2023/C 340/09 (27 September 2023).

Holders of diplomatic, service/official and special passports issued by Tunisia are exempt from Schengen visa requirements for Spain. Bilateral agreement recorded in EU OJ 2023/C 340/09 (27 September 2023).

Holders of diplomatic passports issued by Uzbekistan are exempt from visa requirements for Spain (diplomatic passports only; service passports NOT included per the Council of Ministers authorization text of February 2025). Spain's Council of Ministers authorized signing of the reciprocal exemption agreement in February 2025 - see thediplomatinspain.com report. Formal BOE publication pending ratification; BOE entry not confirmed as of June 2026. Retain with caution pending BOE publication.

visa_free.name: EU / EEA member states (all 27 EU + Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway) + Switzerland

eta.name: United Kingdom (ETA required since April 2025, mandatory enforcement February 2026)
United States (ESTA required under Visa Waiver Program)

outbound_official.notes: Spain's Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Ministerio de Asuntos Exteriores, Unión Europea y Cooperación) publishes outbound travel guidance for Spanish citizens through per-country 'Recomendaciones de viaje' (Travel Recommendations) pages at exteriores.gob.es. There is no single consolidated aggregate visa-free list equivalent to India's mea.gov.in/vffin; visa/entry requirements for Spanish citizens are embedded within individual country advisories. The visa_free list above is derived from EU freedom-of-movement rights (EU/EEA/CH) plus the destinations confirmed visa-free on individual MFA country pages and per the inbound EU Regulation 2018/1806 Annex II reciprocity (confirmed on official per-country pages, e.g. Japan page explicitly states 'No es necesario visado para estancias de hasta 90 días'). Several nominally 'visa-free' destinations require electronic travel authorisation (ETA/ESTA) prior to travel - these are listed in the eta array. The UK ETA became mandatory from February 25 2026 per the Spain London Consulate official advisory. Russian e-visa is available since August 2023 per the Russia country recommendation page. As of June 2026, ETIAS (Schengen ETA for visa-free third-country nationals entering Spain) remains pending launch.

cbi.notes: Spain does not offer a citizenship-by-investment program. Spanish citizenship is obtained by naturalization after 10 years of legal residence (2 years for nationals of Latin American countries, Philippines, Andorra, Equatorial Guinea, Portugal, or Sephardic Jews). The investor residence visa ('Golden Visa') under Law 14/2013 was abolished on April 3, 2025 by Organic Law 1/2025. No CBI route exists in Spanish law. Source: https://sede.mjusticia.gob.es/en/tramites/nacionalidad-espanola and https://www.exteriores.gob.es/Consulados/nuevayork/en/ServiciosConsulares/Paginas/Consular/Visado-de-inversor.aspx

programs.name: Investor Residence Visa / Golden Visa (ABOLISHED)
programs.notes: ABOLISHED on April 3, 2025 by Organic Law 1/2025 (published BOE January 3, 2025), which eliminated articles 63-67 of Law 14/2013. Investment options were: real estate ≥€500,000 (unencumbered); shares/stocks in Spanish companies ≥€1,000,000; Spanish public debt ≥€2,000,000; business projects of general interest. Applications submitted before April 3, 2025 may still be processed under prior rules. Min_amount reflects the former real estate option (lowest threshold). Citizenship path is via standard naturalization (10 years residence generally).

programs.name: Non-Lucrative Residence Visa
programs.notes: For non-EU nationals who wish to reside in Spain without engaging in work activities. Requires proof of sufficient economic means (400% of monthly IPREM for the applicant, plus 100% per dependent). Initial visa valid 1 year; can be renewed. After 5 years continuous legal residence may apply for long-term residence permit (permanent residence). Path to citizenship is 10 years (2 years for certain nationalities). No minimum investment amount required - based on income/savings proof.

programs.name: Telework Visa (Digital Nomad Visa)
programs.eligibility: Non-EU nationals working remotely for companies located outside Spain. Must hold a university degree from a prestigious institution or have 3+ years of professional experience. Employees must work exclusively for non-Spanish companies; self-employed may work up to 20% for Spanish entities.
programs.notes: Established under Law 28/2022 (Startup Law) amending Law 14/2013. Visa valid up to 1 year; can be converted to residence authorization (up to 3 years) via UGE-CE. Income threshold: 200% of monthly SMI (€2,442/month as of 2026). Processed by UGE-CE (Unidad de Grandes Empresas y Colectivos Estratégicos).

programs.name: Entrepreneur Visa (Startup/Innovative Project)
programs.eligibility: Non-EU nationals planning to launch an innovative business project of particular economic interest for Spain. Requires a favourable report from the Economic and Commercial Office evaluating job creation, qualifications, and economic value.
programs.notes: Established under Law 14/2013 as amended by Startup Law 28/2022. Financial requirement: 100% of IPREM plus 50% per dependent. Visa valid 1 year. After arrival, can obtain entrepreneur residence authorization through UGE-CE. Covered by Spain's 'Entrepreneurs Law'.

programs.name: Highly Qualified Worker Visa / EU Blue Card
programs.eligibility: Non-EU nationals with a valid employment contract for highly qualified work. Must hold a university/college degree (3+ years) or 5+ years of professional experience. Minimum gross annual salary €38,844 (2024); reduced threshold €31,075 for shortage occupations or recent graduates.
programs.notes: Two routes: Spain's national Highly Qualified Worker visa (Law 14/2013) and the EU Blue Card. EU Blue Card valid 3 years, renewable for 2 years. EU Blue Card fee: €73.26 initial application, €16.08 card issuance. Processed through UGE-CE at inclusion.gob.es. Visa initially valid 1 year pending residence authorization.

programs.name: Intra-Company Transfer Visa
programs.eligibility: Non-EU nationals transferring within the same company or group of companies to Spain as senior management, specialists, or trainees.
programs.processing_time: 10 days from submission (visa); 20 working days for residence authorization
programs.notes: Established under Law 14/2013. Visa valid 1 year or duration of residence permit if shorter. Requires Initial Residence Authorization from Spain's Dirección General de Migraciones. Family members may apply concurrently.

visa_types.name: Schengen Short-Stay Tourist Visa (Type C)
visa_types.purpose: Permits leisure travel within the Schengen area for tourism, family visits, medical treatment, or other non-gainful activities for up to 90 days in any 180-day period.
visa_types.notes: Fee is $106 for most nationals age 12+; $53 for ages 6-11; free for under 5. Reduced rates apply for select Eastern European countries (e.g. $41 for Albania, Armenia, Belarus, Georgia, Moldova, Montenegro, North Macedonia, Serbia, Ukraine). BLS service charge of $20 additional. Financial proof required: €122/day (under 9 days) or €1,099 minimum. Travel insurance mandatory (min €30,000). Single or multiple entries available. Apply 15-180 days before travel.

visa_types.name: Schengen Short-Stay Business Visa (Type C)
visa_types.purpose: For business travelers and self-employed individuals conducting business activities, attending conferences, or visiting trade fairs in Spain for up to 90 days in any 180-day period.
visa_types.notes: Same fee structure as tourist Schengen visa ($106 general; $53 ages 6-11; free under 6). BLS service fee $20 additional. Requires employer letter or Spanish company invitation, travel insurance, and proof of financial means. Apply at least 15 days before travel.

visa_types.name: Airport Transit Visa (Type A)
visa_types.purpose: Allows travelers from certain nationalities to transit through the international transit zone of Spanish airports without exiting into Schengen territory.
visa_types.notes: Only required for certain nationalities; many are exempt. Maximum validity 6 months. Single, double or multiple entries. Fee: $106 for most nationals age 12+; $53 for ages 6-11; free under 5. BLS service charge $20 additional. Not required if traveler holds a valid Schengen visa, residence permit, or certain other visas.

visa_types.name: Transit Visa for Seamen
visa_types.purpose: Permits maritime crew members to transit through Spain for the purpose of joining a ship or in transit after leaving a ship.
visa_types.notes: Short-term visa category for maritime workers in transit. Same general fee structure as other short-stay visas.

visa_types.name: Short-Stay Visa for Family Members of EU/EEA/Swiss Citizens (Type C)
visa_types.purpose: Allows non-EU family members of EU/EEA/Swiss citizens to accompany or join them in Spain for stays up to 90 days in any 180-day period.
visa_types.notes: Free of charge. Eligible family members include spouse, registered partner, dependent children under 21, parents, and other qualifying relatives. For stays exceeding 90 days, a Residence Card as Union Citizen Family Member is required instead.

visa_types.name: Residence Visa for Family Members of Spanish Citizens
visa_types.purpose: Long-stay national visa enabling non-EU family members of Spanish nationals to relocate to Spain for residence exceeding 90 days.
visa_types.notes: Free of charge. Visa valid 1 year; upon arrival in Spain applicant must apply for Foreigner Identity Card (TIE) within one month. Processing may take up to 2 months due to inter-authority consultations. Eligible relatives include spouses, partners, children under 26, parents, legal guardians, and caregivers.

visa_types.name: Study Visa (National Long-Stay Visa for Studies)
visa_types.purpose: National long-stay visa for higher education, post-secondary studies, student mobility programs, volunteering, and training activities in Spain for stays exceeding 90 days.
visa_types.notes: Fee for US citizens $160; most other nationalities $106; UK citizens $106 (under 180 days) or $462 (over 180 days); Australian citizens $472. BLS service fee $20 additional. Visa valid 1 month before program start and 15 days after program end. For stays exceeding 180 days, TIE must be obtained within 30 days of arrival. Financial requirement: €600/month solo student. Apply 2-6 months before program start. Medical certificate required. New RD 1155/2024 effective May 20, 2025.

visa_types.name: NALCAP Visa (North American Language and Culture Assistants Program)
visa_types.purpose: Study visa for participants in Spain's Ministry of Education Language and Culture Assistant program, enabling language teaching assistants to reside in Spain for the program duration.
visa_types.notes: Fee for US citizens $160; most nationalities $106; UK citizens $106 (under 180 days) or $462 (over 180 days); Australian citizens $472. Apply at least 2 months before program start. Processing takes minimum 5 weeks. For stays over 180 days, TIE required within 30 days of arrival.

visa_types.name: Au Pair Visa
visa_types.purpose: Allows young adults aged 17-30 to live with a Spanish host family for cultural and language exchange, providing household assistance in return for accommodation and stipend.
visa_types.notes: Age requirement: 17-30 years. Must be enrolled in accredited Spanish language program. Services capped at 5 hours daily; one full day off per week. Financial proof: €600/month minimum. Fee: US citizens $160; Australian citizens $472; UK citizens $462; most others $106. BLS service fee $20. Processing approximately 1 month.

visa_types.name: Residence and Work Authorization - Employee (Cuenta Ajena)
visa_types.purpose: Authorizes non-EU nationals to reside and work in Spain as an employee for a Spanish employer, subject to the national employment situation assessment.
visa_types.notes: Employer must apply for the authorization; applicant then applies for the national work and residence visa at a Spanish consulate. Initial authorization valid 1 year. Seasonal work and cross-border worker variants also exist. Governed by Organic Law 4/2000 (Foreigners Law) and implementing Royal Decree 557/2011.

visa_types.name: Highly Qualified Worker Visa (TAC) / EU Blue Card
visa_types.purpose: Enables highly qualified non-EU professionals or senior managers to work in Spain, including the EU Blue Card for high-salary positions requiring tertiary education.
visa_types.notes: Requires prior authorization from UGECE (Large Companies and Strategic Groups Unit). Fee: US citizens $190; most others $106; Canadian $992; UK $963; Australian $3,086. BLS service fee $20. Visa valid 1 year or matching residence authorization. Must hold higher education degree or 3+ years equivalent professional experience. EU Blue Card minimum salary threshold approx. €38,844/year (shortage occupations lower). Residence authorization processed by UGECE.

visa_types.name: Intra-Company Transfer Visa (TTI)
visa_types.purpose: Permits employees of multinational companies to be transferred to their company's Spanish entity as senior management, specialists, or trainees.
visa_types.notes: Requires prior residence authorization from UGECE. Fee: US citizens $190; most others $106; Canadian $992; UK $963; Australian $3,086. BLS service fee $20. Visa valid 1 year or duration of residence authorization. Established under Law 14/2013. Family members may apply concurrently.

visa_types.name: Researcher Visa
visa_types.purpose: Allows non-EU nationals to conduct research, development, and innovation activities at authorized Spanish public or private entities.
visa_types.notes: Requires Authorization of Initial Residence for Research from UGECE. Fee: US citizens $190; most others $106; BLS service fee $20. Visa valid 1 year or matching authorization. Covers research staff, scientific/technical personnel, teaching staff at R&D centers and universities. EU researcher mobility provisions apply (Sheet 69 for mobility between EU member states).

visa_types.name: Internship Visa
visa_types.purpose: For university graduates or current university students to participate in approved internship programs at Spanish entities to improve professional skills and experience.
visa_types.notes: Must have obtained university degree within 2 years or be pursuing degree. Requires Authorization of Initial Residence for Internship from provincial government. Fee: US citizens $190; most others $106; Canadian $992; Australian $3,086; BLS $20. Maximum duration: 6 months (hosting agreement) or 1 year (trainee contract).

visa_types.name: Residence Visa with Work Permit Exemption
visa_types.purpose: Authorizes specific categories of professionals (academics, researchers, cultural staff, foreign correspondents, religious ministers, etc.) to work in Spain without a standard work permit.
visa_types.notes: Covers: technical specialists at Spanish government/university/research organizations, educators at Spanish universities, cultural/educational staff from recognized foreign institutions, foreign officials under bilateral cooperation agreements, foreign media correspondents, scientific mission members, religious ministers, international trade union representatives, and foreign minors in protective services. Three durations: up to 90 days (Schengen code EET), 90-180 days (national code TEE), over 180 days (TRE). Fee: US citizens $190; most $106; Canadian $1,050; Australian $3,235; UK $414; residence fee $13 all. Processing 3 months. Authority: Article 117 of Royal Decree 557/2011.

visa_types.name: Telework Visa (Digital Nomad Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Allows non-EU nationals to reside in Spain while working remotely for companies or clients located exclusively outside Spain, using digital and telecommunications means.
visa_types.notes: Established under Law 28/2022 (Startup Law), amending Law 14/2013, Article 74 bis. Income requirement: 200% of monthly SMI = €2,368/month (approx. $2,500) for solo applicant; add €888/month per first family member, €196/month per additional. Must hold university degree from recognized institution or 3+ years professional experience. Employees must work for non-Spanish companies only; self-employed may work up to 20% for Spanish entities. Work must be for companies (not individuals, NGOs, or government bodies). Fee: US citizens $190; Canadian $1,566; UK $963; Australian $3,020; most others $106. Can convert to 3-year residence permit via UGECE without leaving Spain. Processing 10 days.

visa_types.name: Entrepreneur Visa
visa_types.purpose: Visa for non-EU nationals relocating to Spain to undertake an innovative entrepreneurial activity of particular economic interest to Spain.
visa_types.notes: Established under Law 14/2013 as amended by Law 28/2022 (Startup Law). Requires prior Authorization of Initial Residence from UGECE evaluating innovativeness and economic interest. Financial requirement: $8,000/year for solo applicant plus $4,000/year per dependent (2025 figures = 100% IPREM). Health insurance with 100% coverage required. FBI background check required. Fee: US citizens $270; Canadian $789; most others $106. Visa valid 1 year; convertible to entrepreneur residence authorization. Must apply within 1 year of capital investment (excluding real estate).

visa_types.name: Non-Lucrative Residence Visa
visa_types.purpose: Permits non-EU nationals with sufficient passive income or savings to reside in Spain without engaging in any gainful employment or professional activities.
visa_types.notes: Income requirement: 400% of Spain's IPREM = €28,800/year (~$32,000) for solo applicant; add 100% IPREM (~$8,000) per dependent (2026 figures). Visa itself valid 90 days from issuance (must enter Spain within this window). Initial residence permit valid 1 year upon arrival. Renewable; after 5 years of continuous legal residence, may apply for long-term/permanent residence permit. Path to citizenship: 10 years (2 years for nationals of Latin American countries, Philippines, Andorra, Equatorial Guinea, Portugal). Visa fee varies by nationality: $106-$789; residence permit fee $13; BLS $20. Processing up to 3 months. No work authorization; any income must be passive. Governed by Article 37 of Royal Decree 557/2011.

visa_types.name: Self-Employment / Entrepreneur Residence Authorization (Cuenta Propia)
visa_types.purpose: Authorizes non-EU non-residents to establish themselves as self-employed workers in Spain or initiate a business project.
visa_types.notes: Initial temporary residence and self-employment work authorization (Sheet 14). Also includes job search or business project initiation authorization (Sheet 20). Must not already be a legal resident in Spain. Requires viable business plan or self-employment documentation. Governed by Organic Law 4/2000 and Royal Decree 557/2011.

agreements: EU freedom of movement (EU citizens and EEA/Swiss equivalents)
Schengen Area (free movement within Schengen zone)

validation_notes: CORRECTION (June 2026 validation): Added Albania (ALB) to visa_free list - it was erroneously omitted. Albania has been in EU Regulation 2018/1806 Annex II since 2010 (biometric passports only, 90 days/180 days). Verified against consolidated regulation text (2025-12-30).

CORRECTION (June 2026 validation): Updated ETIAS eta note from 'expected late 2026' to reflect revised timeline: soft launch Q4 2026, mandatory enforcement not before April 2027 per EU official revised timeline and news reports January 2026.
CORRECTION (June 2026 validation): Updated visa_policy.notes to mention Vanuatu removal (effective 14 Jan 2025, Reg. EU 2025/11) and Albania addition, providing context for Annex II changes.
CONFIRMED: Morocco is correctly absent from visa_free list (removed from Annex II; remains in conditional_access for diplomatic/service/special passports only).
CONFIRMED: Vanuatu is correctly absent from visa_free list (removed effective 14 January 2025 by Regulation EU 2025/11).
CONFIRMED: Spain's Golden Visa (investor residence) abolished April 3, 2025 by Organic Law 1/2025. Record correctly reflects this.
CONFIRMED: Digital nomad visa income threshold of €2,442/month is correct for 2026 (200% of 2026 SMI of €1,221/month, per BOE publication February 2026).
CONFIRMED: EU Regulation 2018/1806 consolidated version date 2025-12-30 is correct and most recent as of validation date.

CORRECTION (2026-06-27 re-validation): Kosovo entry in visa_free updated to specify 'biometric passports only' per EU Regulation 2023/850 text (visa-free applies only to biometric passports issued in line with ICAO standards). Previous note omitted this condition. Source: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/HTML/?uri=CELEX:32023R0850

CORRECTION (2026-06-27 re-validation): ETA entry updated to note EES (Entry/Exit System) is now fully operational as of 10 April 2026 across all Schengen external borders. ETIAS remains separate and not yet operational (Q4 2026 expected). Source: https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/news/entryexit-system-ees-fully-operational-2026-04-10_en

CORRECTION (2026-06-27 re-validation): visa_policy.notes updated to mention Kosovo addition (Reg. 2023/850, effective 1 January 2024, biometric passports) and EES full operational date (10 April 2026).

CORRECTION (2026-06-27 re-validation): Uzbekistan conditional_access note clarified that the February 2025 Council of Ministers authorization covers diplomatic passports ONLY (not service passports), per thediplomatinspain.com report on the authorization text.

CORRECTION (2026-06-27 re-validation): Saudi Arabia conditional_access note updated to confirm agreement is titled 'Agreement on the Mutual Visa Exemption for Holders of Diplomatic, Special and Service Passports', signed 15 May 2026, covering diplomatic, service, AND special passports. Confirmed by Saudi Gazette, Zawya, and Arabia Business reports. BOE publication pending.

CONFIRMED (2026-06-27): All other Annex II countries in visa_free list verified against EU Regulation 2018/1806 consolidated text (2025-12-30) fetched from EUR-Lex. No additions or removals required beyond Kosovo biometric qualifier.

CONFIRMED (2026-06-27): Kosovo visa-free access started 1 January 2024 per home-affairs.ec.europa.eu official announcement - not contingent on ETIAS (the 'whichever comes first' clause meant 1 January 2024 triggered first).
CONFIRMED (2026-06-27): ETIAS Q4 2026 launch with mandatory enforcement not before April 2027 remains current per EU official sources.

CORRECTION (2026-06-29 re-validation): Georgia diplomatic/service/official passport visa-free CONFIRMED SUSPENDED effective March 6, 2026 to March 6, 2027, per Commission Implementing Regulation under revised Visa Suspension Mechanism (Regulation 2025/2441, first application). These passport-type bilateral waivers had already been omitted from conditional_access as unverifiable in prior pass. The suspension does not affect ordinary Georgian biometric passport holders, who remain on Annex II visa-free list and are confirmed correct. Gap note for Georgia updated accordingly. Sources: home-affairs.ec.europa.eu; EEAS announcement 2026-03-06.

CONFIRMED (2026-06-29): EES fully operational April 10, 2026 across all 29 Schengen countries (confirmed via home-affairs.ec.europa.eu announcement). ETIAS still expected Q4 2026, mandatory enforcement 6 months after EES full implementation = approximately April 2027.

CONFIRMED (2026-06-29): Georgia ordinary biometric passport holders remain in visa_free list per Annex II EU Regulation 2018/1806 (confirmed current; suspension only covers diplomatic/service/official passports).

CONFIRMED (2026-06-29): No new countries added to EU Regulation 2018/1806 Annex II in 2025-2026 beyond Kosovo (effective 1 January 2024). Vanuatu removal (Regulation 2025/11, effective 14 January 2025) confirmed already absent from visa_free list.
gaps: Annex I of EU Regulation 2018/1806 (visa-required countries) was not fully enumerated in this record - official EUR-Lex consolidated text used as authoritative source for Annex II (visa-free); Annex I countries confirmed to include all non-Annex-II countries per the regulation structure.
Exact path-to-permanent-residence years for Non-Lucrative Visa not confirmed from official page (5-year standard EU long-term residence rule applied).
EU Blue Card salary threshold sourced from EC migration portal (official EU source, not Spain MFA directly); 2025 figure €38,844 cited - 2026 update not confirmed.
ETIAS soft launch Q4 2026 - exact date not yet announced. Mandatory enforcement expected April 2027 at earliest; not yet operational as of June 2026.
No citizenship-by-investment (CBI) program exists for Spain - confirmed from multiple official sources.
Spain's Golden Visa (investor residence) was abolished April 3, 2025 - some consular pages may not yet be fully updated to reflect this.

No official source found for Spain conditional entry based on holding a US visa (B1/B2 or other category) or US Green Card alone - holding a US visa does NOT grant visa-free access to Spain for nationals who otherwise require a Schengen visa.
India OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) card: no Spain-specific official rule found granting entry to Spain based on OCI status. No entry added.

Spain-Saudi Arabia diplomatic/service/special passport agreement signed 15 May 2026 - BOE publication reference not yet found. Agreement confirmed by multiple news sources (Saudi Gazette, Zawya, Arabian Business). Retain in conditional_access with caution pending BOE publication.

Spain-Uzbekistan diplomatic passport agreement (February 2025): Council of Ministers authorized signing (diplomatic passports only). Awaiting BOE publication. Exact entry into force date not confirmed as of June 2026.

EU OJ 2023/C 340/09 (27 September 2023) is the most recent available consolidated list of Spain's bilateral diplomatic/service passport waivers; any agreements concluded after that date (Oman 2026, Saudi Arabia 2026, Uzbekistan 2025) may not yet appear in an EU OJ update.
Complete list of countries with bilateral agreements for diplomatic passport waivers with Spain may have been updated since the September 2023 EU OJ publication.

Georgia diplomatic/service/official passport waivers: CONFIRMED SUSPENDED (2026-06-29 validation). The EU issued a Commission Implementing Regulation temporarily suspending visa-free travel for holders of Georgian diplomatic, service, and official passports, effective March 6, 2026 to March 6, 2027, under the revised Visa Suspension Mechanism. This is the first application of the reinforced suspension mechanism (Regulation 2025/2441). ORDINARY Georgian biometric passport holders remain visa-free (correctly listed in visa_free). The bilateral diplomatic/service passport exemption previously recorded in EU OJ 2023/C 340/09 was never added to conditional_access (already a gap), and is now also suspended EU-wide. No entry added to conditional_access; suspension confirmed. Source: https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/commission-implementing-regulation-temporary-suspension-visa-exemption-georgian-nationals-holding_en and https://ec.europa.eu/commission/presscorner/detail/en/ip_26_564

EES (Entry/Exit System) impact: now fully operational since 10 April 2026. No change to visa classification - EES is a border record system, not a visa requirement. All Annex II visa-free entries remain correct. ETIAS (advance authorization) still not launched.
